title: ferray-ufunc: add_reduce collects all data into flat Vec before reducing — O(2n) memory
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

add_reduce() in arithmetic.rs does 'let data: Vec<T> = input.iter().copied().collect()' to flatten the array into a Vec, then manually computes the reduction via nested loops over the flat data. This doubles memory usage and ignores the array's strides. NumPy's reduction.c works directly on the strided array via nditer, using no extra memory. The fix: iterate over the array's lanes directly using ndarray's .lanes(axis) or .axis_iter(axis), accumulating in-place without flattening.
